venue: IEEE Transactions on Neural Networks and Learning Systems
abstract_inverted_index: Deep learning has achieved remarkable success in numerous domains with help from large amounts of big data. However, the quality data labels is a concern because lack high-quality many real-world scenarios. As noisy severely degrade generalization performance deep neural networks, (robust training) becoming an important task modern applications. In this survey, we first describe problem label noise supervised perspective. Next, provide comprehensive review 62 state-of-the-art robust training methods, all which are categorized into five groups according to their methodological difference, followed by systematic comparison six properties used evaluate superiority. Subsequently, perform in-depth analysis rate estimation and summarize typically evaluation methodology, including public datasets metrics. Finally, present several promising research directions that can serve as guideline for future studies.

venue: IEEE Transactions on Knowledge and Data Engineering
abstract_inverted_index: Generative adversarial networks (GANs) have recently become a hot research topic; however, they been studied since 2014, and large number of algorithms proposed. Nevertheless, few comprehensive studies explain the connections among different GAN variants how evolved. In this paper, we attempt to provide review various methods from perspectives algorithms, theory, applications. First, motivations, mathematical representations, structures most are introduced in detail, compare their commonalities differences. Second, theoretical issues related GANs investigated. Finally, typical applications image processing computer vision, natural language processing, music, speech audio, medical field, data science discussed.

venue: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence
abstract_inverted_index: Contextual information is vital in visual understanding problems, such as semantic segmentation and object detection. We propose a criss-cross network (CCNet) for obtaining full-image contextual very effective efficient way. Concretely, each pixel, novel attention module harvests the of all pixels on its path. By taking further recurrent operation, pixel can finally capture dependencies. Besides, category consistent loss proposed to enforce produce more discriminative features. Overall, CCNet with following merits: 1) GPU memory friendly. Compared non-local block, requires 11× less usage. 2) High computational efficiency. The significantly reduces FLOPs by about 85 percent block. 3) state-of-the-art performance. conduct extensive experiments benchmarks including Cityscapes, ADE20K, human parsing benchmark LIP, instance COCO, video CamVid. In particular, our achieves mIoU scores 81.9, 45.76 55.47 Cityscapes test set, ADE20K validation set LIP respectively, which are new results. source codes available at https://github.com/speedinghzl/CCNethttps://github.com/speedinghzl/CCNet.

abstract_inverted_index: ChatGPT, an artificial intelligence generated content (AIGC) model developed by OpenAI, has attracted world-wide attention for its capability of dealing with challenging language understanding and generation tasks in the form conversations. This paper briefly provides overview on history, status quo potential future development helping to provide entry point think about ChatGPT. Specifically, from limited open-accessed resources, we conclude core techniques mainly including large-scale models, in-context learning, reinforcement learning human feedback key technical steps developing Chat-GPT. We further analyze pros cons ChatGPT rethink duality various fields. Although it been widely acknowledged that brings plenty opportunities fields, mankind should still treat use properly avoid threat, e.g., academic integrity safety challenge. venue: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence
abstract_inverted_index: Transformer with self-attention has led to the revolutionizing of natural language processing field, and recently inspires emergence Transformer-style architecture design competitive results in numerous computer vision tasks. Nevertheless, most existing designs directly employ over a 2D feature map obtain attention matrix based on pairs isolated queries keys at each spatial location, but leave rich contexts among neighbor under-exploited. In this work, we novel module, i.e., Contextual (CoT) block, for visual recognition. Such fully capitalizes contextual information input guide learning dynamic thus strengthens capacity representation. Technically, CoT block first contextually encodes via 3×3 convolution, leading static representation inputs. We further concatenate encoded learn multi-head through two consecutive 1×1 convolutions. The learnt is multiplied by values achieve fusion representations are finally taken as outputs. Our appealing view that it can readily replace convolution ResNet architectures, yielding backbone named Networks (CoTNet). Through extensive experiments wide range applications (e.g., image recognition, object detection, instance segmentation, semantic segmentation), validate superiority CoTNet stronger backbone. Source code available https://github.com/JDAI-CV/CoTNet.

abstract_inverted_index: Many NLP applications require manual text annotations for a variety of tasks, notably to train classifiers or evaluate the performance unsupervised models. Depending on size and degree complexity, tasks may be conducted by crowd workers platforms such as MTurk well trained annotators, research assistants. Using four samples tweets news articles ( n = 6,183), we show that ChatGPT outperforms several annotation including relevance, stance, topics, frame detection. Across datasets, zero-shot accuracy exceeds about 25 percentage points average, while ChatGPT’s intercoder agreement both annotators all tasks. Moreover, per-annotation cost is less than $0.003—about thirty times cheaper MTurk. These results demonstrate potential large language models drastically increase efficiency classification.

title: StrongSORT: Make DeepSORT Great Again

abstract_inverted_index: Recently, Multi-Object Tracking (MOT) has attracted rising attention, and accordingly, remarkable progresses have been achieved. However, the existing methods tend to use various basic models (e.g, detector embedding model), different training or inference tricks, etc. As a result, construction of good baseline for fair comparison is essential. In this paper, classic tracker, i.e., DeepSORT, first revisited, then significantly improved from multiple perspectives such as object detection, feature embedding, trajectory association. The proposed named StrongSORT, contributes strong MOT community. Moreover, two lightweight plug-and-play algorithms are address inherent "missing" problems MOT: missing association detection. Specifically, unlike most methods, which associate short tracklets into complete trajectories at high computation complexity, we propose an appearance-free link model (AFLink) perform global without appearance information, achieve balance between speed accuracy. Furthermore, Gaussian-smoothed interpolation (GSI) based on Gaussian process regression relieve AFLink GSI can be easily plugged trackers with negligible extra computational cost (1.7 ms 7.1 per image, respectively, MOT17). Finally, by fusing StrongSORT GSI, final tracker (StrongSORT++) achieves state-of-the-art results public benchmarks, MOT17, MOT20, DanceTrack KITTI.
